title: 6.5 B+ Tree
subtitle: Leaf-linked indexed search tree for range queries

content.introduction: Multi-way balanced trees generalize binary branching to reduce height and optimize block-oriented storage access.

sections.title: 6.5.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define node degree, key ranges, and balanced-height guarantees in multi-way search trees.
examples: Interpret key interval routing in an internal node
Explain why higher branching reduces height

sections.title: 6.5.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe node split/merge/redistribution behavior during insertion and deletion.
examples: Insert keys until a node split occurs
Propagate split to parent and possibly root
Handle underflow with borrow or merge

sections.title: 6.5.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Discuss I/O efficiency benefits for external memory and why database indexes prefer multi-way trees.
examples: Estimate disk page accesses by tree height
Explain why B+ leaves are linked for ranges
Use multi-way trees for large persistent indexes

keyTakeaways: Connect fan-out to lower tree height
Prepare for detailed B-Tree and B+ Tree operations
